title: Cross-GPU BAR0 Comparison: K80 (GK210/Kepler) vs Titan V (GV100/Volta)

k80.warm_regs: not analyzed (driver init was part of cold capture)

titanv.sec2_acr_visible: 5 ACR + 0 SEC2 regs (rest hidden behind falcon DMA)
titanv.sovereign_readiness: MEDIUM - VBIOS recipe captured, but SEC2/ACR falcon init requires signed microcode

cross_comparison.note: 98.5% of shared offsets have different values (expected: different architectures)

key_findings: K80 PGRAPH: 2048 regs initialized by VBIOS (no falcon gates)
Titan V PGRAPH: 0 regs in cold (all BADF) — requires FECS/GPCCS falcon init
Titan V SEC2/ACR: almost entirely inaccessible through BAR0 MMIO (falcon DMA window)
Titan V nvidia-535 changes only 255 regs on top of VBIOS — surprisingly small delta
Titan V FECS cold: 42 real regs among 873 raw (831 BADF excluded)
K80 has no PMU/FECS/GPCCS/ACR/SEC2 in its BAR0 recipe — these engines don't exist or are uninitialized in Kepler
next_steps: K80: Attempt sovereign cold-boot by replaying 10283-register recipe from coral-driver
Titan V: Extract SEC2/ACR falcon microcode from nvidia-535 .run package
Titan V: Map PRAMIN window to read falcon DMEM/IMEM for SEC2 bootstrapping
Both: Build register replay engine in coral-driver for automated init
